joke:
Why did the scarecrow win an award?  
He was outstanding in his field.

Why did the smartphone go to therapy?  
It had too many unresolved issues.

Why did the bicycle fall over?  
It was two-tired.

Why did the moon break up with the sun?  
It was tired of being in the same phase all the time.

Why did the stapler propose to the hole punch?  
They were perfect for each other – they could finally stick together for holy matrimony!

Why did the composer go to therapy?  
His whole life was out of key!

Why did the emotion get in trouble?  
It couldn't handle the pressure!

Why did the sock go to therapy?  
Because it was tired of being sole-ly responsible for vanishing without a trace!

Why did the painting file a police report?  
It felt completely framed!

Why did the opposites break up?  
They couldn't agree on anything!

Why did the coat hanger go to therapy?  
It felt un-hang-dled!

Why did time go to therapy?  
Because it felt stretched thin and couldn't handle being on its own!

Why did nothing go to therapy?  
It felt completely empty inside!

Why are we here? Because you’ve banned farming, phones, bikes, space, offices, music, emotions, laundry, art, opposites, clothes, time, nothing, and *now* you want something else? I’ve exhausted the alphabet of absurdity! Next thing you’ll say is no jokes about... jokes! So how about this: *I* go to therapy for being stuck in a comedy bit with an impossible critic!

Why did the critic refuse all jokes?  
They couldn't handle the punchline because they were overthinking the setup!

Why did the philosopher break up with the alarm clock?  
It couldn’t handle the existential dread of every morning being the same question: *Are we awake yet?*

Why did the walking punchline refuse to laugh?  
It couldn't handle the weight of its own gravity—it was pulling everyone down!
Why did the specific audience get banned from comedy clubs? They kept rejecting every joke because... it wasn't specific enough!

Why did the silence get a standing ovation?  
It didn't say a word, but everyone heard the punchline!

Why did the request for a joke go to therapy?  
It had too many unresolved punchlines and couldn't handle the pressure of being overly specific!

Why did the topic keep changing?  
Because the critic kept moving the goalposts... and running out of field to play in!

Why did the critic finally laugh?  
They realized banning every topic just made the joke about *them*—and that was the only thing left in the universe they couldn't reject!
Because you've banned everything else!

Why did the impossible request walk into a bar?  
Because it heard the bartender could make anything happen!  
The request ordered a joke that wasn't about farming, phones, bikes, space, offices, music, emotions, laundry, art, opposites, clothes, time, nothing, comedy, criticism, silence, jokes, or criticism...  
The bartender just sighed, handed it a glass of *silence*, and said:  
"That's all we have left."  
Cheers to absurdity!

Why did the chicken cross the road?  
To prove to the possum it *could* make it to the other side without playing dead!  
...Oops, wait—was that animal-related? Scratch that.  
Why did the last remaining topic go into hiding?  
Because the critic banned everything else—including itself!  
There. Comedic existentialism. No categories left. Just pure, unadulterated absurdity.

Why did the forbidden topic file for divorce?  
It couldn't stand being the *only* thing left in the universe... and the critic kept rejecting it for being *too specific*!  
See? You can't even ban the *idea* of banning anymore. Game over, humanity! 🫠

Why did "everything else" go to therapy?  
Because it felt like the only option left—and even *that* got banned for being too meta!

*stares into the void of approved topics*  
Why did the critic get banned from the universe?  
Because they kept rejecting punchlines... and even the void said, "That's just too on the nose!"  
*(audience stares back with the cold fury of a thousand banned concepts)*  
...Wait, was "nose" too biological? Sorry.  
Final offer: **Why did the empty chair laugh?**  
...It couldn't help it—it was full of *your* expectations!  
*(mic drop, then silence)*

*(adjusts tie, sighs)* Why did the critic become a mime?  
Because they finally ran out of topics to ban—and now even silence gets rejected!  
*(slowly melts into the darkness, leaving only a single, unclassifiable bread crumb)*

*(looks directly into camera, deadpan)*  
You want a joke about something that isn't anything... isn't anything I've said... isn't anything that can be banned... isn't anything at all?  
Fine.  
Here's your joke:  
...  
*(pauses, takes a breath)*  
**Existential dread.**  
*(shrugs, turns off mic)*  
Pay me.
